fundamentals.name: MongoDB, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MongoDB, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides general purpose database platform worldwide. The company offers MongoDB Atlas, a hosted multi-cloud database-as-a-service solution; MongoDB Enterprise Advanced, a commercial database server for enterprise customers to run in the cloud, on-premises, or in a hybrid environment; and Community Server, a free-to-download version of its database, which includes the functionality that developers need to get started with MongoDB. It offers professional services comprising consulting and training. The company was formerly known as 10gen, Inc. and changed its name to MongoDB, Inc. in August 2013. MongoDB, Inc. was incorporated in 2007 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and free cash flow, but currently unprofitable on a net basis and trading at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 12.1%; next earnings date is 2026-05-28.
positives: Revenue growth 26.7% YoY is robust, exceeding analyst estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year.
Free cash flow of $463.32M TTM indicates strong operational cash generation.
Gross margin of 71.7% is healthy, suggesting good pricing power on its core product.
Consensus analyst rating is 'buy' with a mean target price suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Profit margin is negative at -2.9%, and operating margin is 0.0%, indicating the company is not yet profitable.
Forward P/E of 45.89 and PEG ratio of 1.67 suggest a high valuation relative to earnings growth.
Debt/Equity ratio of 2.13 is relatively high, though current and quick ratios indicate good short-term liquidity.
Quarterly earnings growth was negative (-1.9%) in the last reported quarter.

news.paragraph: MongoDB's stock saw a notable dip despite broader market gains, with Bank of America adjusting its price target ahead of the upcoming earnings report. Analysts at BMO Capital maintain a positive outlook, citing the company's strong position in the database market and durable growth potential, while Simply Wall St. highlights the potential competitive advantage from its AI data push. The market is closely watching how MongoDB's Q1 earnings will stack up against revenue and EPS estimates, with the next earnings date set for May 28th.
